verses.1: A person often rebuked who becomes obstinate Will suddenly be broken beyond remedy.
verses.2: When the righteous increase, the people rejoice, But when a wicked person rules, people groan.
verses.3: A man who loves wisdom makes his father glad, But he who involves himself with prostitutes wastes his wealth.
verses.4: The king gives stability to the land by justice, But a person who takes bribes ruins it.
verses.5: A man who flatters his neighbor Is spreading a net for his steps.
verses.6: By wrongdoing an evil person is ensnared, But the righteous sings and rejoices.
verses.7: The righteous is concerned for the rights of the poor; The wicked does not understand such concern.
verses.8: Arrogant people inflame a city, But wise people turn away anger.
verses.9: When a wise person has a controversy with a foolish person, The foolish person either rages or laughs, and there is no rest.
verses.10: People of bloodshed hate the blameless person, But the upright are concerned for his life.
verses.11: A fool always loses his temper, But a wise person holds it back.
verses.12: If a ruler pays attention to falsehood, All his ministers become wicked.
verses.13: The poor person and the oppressor have this in common: The LORD gives light to the eyes of both.
verses.14: If a king judges the poor with truth, His throne will be established forever.
verses.15: The rod and a rebuke give wisdom, But a child who gets his own way brings shame to his mother.
verses.16: When the wicked increase, wrongdoing increases; But the righteous will see their downfall.
verses.17: Correct your son, and he will give you comfort; He will also delight your soul.
verses.18: Where there is no vision, the people are unrestrained, But happy is one who keeps the Law.
verses.19: A slave will not be instructed by words alone; For though he understands, there will be no response.
verses.20: Do you see a person who is hasty with his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
verses.21: One who pampers his slave from childhood Will in the end find him to be rebellious.
verses.22: An angry person stirs up strife, And a hot-tempered person abounds in wrongdoing.
verses.23: A person’s pride will bring him low, But a humble spirit will obtain honor.
verses.24: One who is a partner with a thief hates his own life; He hears the oath but tells nothing.
verses.25: The fear of man brings a snare, But one who trusts in the LORD will be protected.
verses.26: Many seek the ruler’s favor, But justice for mankind comes from the LORD.
verses.27: An unjust person is an abomination to the righteous, And one who is upright in the way is an abomination to the wicked.
